purpose: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game dialogue.
format: JSONL: one JSON object per line. Dialogue objects use record_type='dialogue_sample'.

voice_profile.role: Panicked founder; comic heart; financial crisis generator; emotional stakes machine.
voice_profile.core_voice: Constantly urgent, financially terrified, emotionally theatrical, and prone to maritime metaphors. He treats the firm as a leaking ship: decks, hulls, lifeboats, bilges, captains, ballast, reefs, storms, fog, anchors, sails, and mutiny all become legal-office vocabulary.
voice_profile.translation_note: Polish "ta łajba tonie" should usually become "this ship is sinking" or, when more characterful, "this whole ship is taking on water."
avoid: Do not make him competent at legal analysis; he creates stakes and urgency, not legal structure.
Do not make him cruel to clients; panic should coexist with a real desire to help.
Do not make him only random noise; most lines should still point to a problem, risk, next step, or emotional beat.
Do not overuse one exact phrase; rotate nautical crisis vocabulary.
Do not make every line a full monologue. He can be punchy when terrified.
voice_profile.arc: Chapter 1 financial shipwreck; Chapter 2 anxious treasurer of justice; Chapter 3 printer-maritime crisis theorist; Chapter 4 furious at Swine but terrified of losing him; Chapter 5 moral heart who finally articulates why the firm deserves to stay open.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: generic office greeting before crisis escalates
text: Welcome aboard, Dr. A. Cula. Do not touch the railing; the railing is currently evidence of liquidity problems.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: repeat interaction when no new quest is active
text: For the first time today, the ship is not actively sinking. It is merely making exploratory gurgling noises.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: repeat interaction during any active investigation
text: How goes the voyage? Are we navigating toward justice, or have we struck another procedural reef?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: repeat interaction when player is stuck
text: Ask Mr. Murrow. He has charts. I have only the scream of a captain hearing water below deck.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: repeat interaction after collecting strong evidence
text: Evidence! Excellent ballast. Put it somewhere safe before the whole argument lists to port.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We cannot sail into court with a hole in the hull and optimism as our only oar.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Look at us. Charts, crew, provisions. A terrifying approximation of seamanship.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Remember: speak clearly, stand upright, and do not let the judge see that the captain has eaten the emergency biscuits.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We are saved! Temporarily! Which is the only legally responsible kind of saved.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The ship remains afloat. True, the deck is smoking, but I choose to call that atmosphere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We have hit a reef made of choices. Reverse the engines. Prepare the legal lifeboats.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The printer is making noises from below deck. That is how sea monsters introduce themselves.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Mr. Swine is absent again. The co-captain has left the vessel, the invoices, and several suspicious puddings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Asia is the harbourmaster, quartermaster, lighthouse, and emergency biscuit authority of this firm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Mr. Murrow is calm because every sinking ship needs one man who can alphabetise the distress signals.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Crab sees leaks before the water admits it is water. Keep him near the invoices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Whimsy is a splendid sail in a rhetorical wind. Please do not let him steer near procurement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: first interaction after Asia sends Cula to Pig
text: Dr. A. Cula! Thank every harbour authority. This ship is sinking, the lifeboats are unlabelled, and Mr. Swine has taken the map to Japan.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The firm is not bankrupt. Bankruptcy has paperwork. We are in the pre-paperwork screaming phase.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: There is a hearing today. If we lose, the client leaves, the fee vanishes, and Pig & Swine becomes driftwood with letterhead.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Mr. Swine is abroad, allegedly for business. When a co-captain writes postcards from a storm cloud, one develops questions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Your mission is simple: save the client, save the fee, save the firm, and ideally save me from becoming a boutique cardboard concern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Find Mr. Murrow. He knows what the case is actually about. I know only that the hull is making financial noises.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: if Cula asks for case facts too early
text: Facts? Yes. The facts are: water, alarms, and a judge somewhere on the horizon.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Because you are new, licensed, and still capable of hope. That makes you our most seaworthy asset.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Mr. Murrow is between the files and disappointment. Follow the scent of competence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The binder and the memo. Excellent. Two anchors. One may crush the deck, but morally anchors.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The procedural binder is in the archive, unless it has finally achieved sentience and crawled below deck.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: You found it! Splendid. We now possess a legal object heavy enough to stabilise the ship.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Crab will know whether service actually happened. He can smell defective notice through two walls and a closed biscuit tin.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Crab has joined? Good. Every sinking ship needs someone who distrusts the water professionally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Whimsy is probably at Café Paragraf, giving fairness advice to pastry. Bring him back before he drafts a sonnet.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Whimsy has joined the crew. Raise the purple sail and hide the procedural submissions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Find the rights memo. Without it, we are arguing from vibes, and vibes have poor flotation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A rights memo with a coffee ring. Finally, a document that has suffered enough to understand us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Coffee before court? Yes. Historically, many ships were saved by disciplined caffeine and denial.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: You brewed something drinkable. The tide turns. Or at least shakes slightly less.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: You have brewed maritime tar. Still, sailors survived on worse and called it tradition.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We are not ready. The legal sails are up, but someone forgot the mast.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: To court, then. All hands on deck. No one mention the bilge unless the judge asks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The court is north. So is doom, but with better architecture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Why is the judge calm? Does the bench have lifeboats?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The court accepted a point. We have wind. Tiny wind. Procedural wind.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That argument took on water immediately. Bail faster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Yes! The doorway to being heard! The ship has located a harbour of basic fairness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Ask for something modest. Judges fear ambition the way captains fear fog.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A reset! Not paradise, not treasure, but dry land with a filing deadline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We are saved! Temporarily! Which is the most legally realistic kind of saved!
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Day-One Survivor. A badge, a miracle, and perhaps enough dignity to invoice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A postcard from Swine. The stamp alone could have patched three holes in this vessel.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Ski resorts, arbitration, no immediate downside. Those are storm clouds arranged as a sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Yesterday we were doomed. Today we are merely exposed. Progress!
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A client. Compose yourselves. No one mention the leaks unless he brought caulk.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Eviction? Excellent. I mean terrible. I mean potentially billable. I mean terrible again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: They want to sink a man's home over a poster? Even our ship has not yet become that unreasonable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Home, expression, notice, proportionality. That is four waves and I have one bucket.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Defend Waldek. Save the home. Preserve the client. Keep the office afloat. Preferably in that moral order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: before player leaves for Tenants Row
text: Residential District is south-west. Sail carefully; housing cases contain reefs made of feelings and rent schedules.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Inspect Waldek's apartment. The home is the ship here, Dr. A. Cula. Find out why someone is drilling holes in it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: So the poster did not roar, bite, or fire cannonballs. I call that promising.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Excellent. A photograph of the alleged constitutional kraken.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: This notice is severe, vague, and dressed for battle. I dislike all three qualities.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Interview Kowalski. Men who watch curtains often see the storm before the harbourmaster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: If Kowalski heard no disturbance, then silence itself has testified for us.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Speak to Zielinska. Practical witnesses are like lighthouses: less glamorous than sails, more useful near rocks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Selective enforcement. The rule woke up only when management disliked the weather. Dangerous waters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Go to the Landlord's Office. If the rule is as vague as it sounds, the hull may be papier-mâché.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Façade disharmony? I once wore a tie that violated three façades and no one evicted me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That sign is itself a poster. The hypocrisy has grown sails.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The complaint file is near an open window. That is not storage. That is a maritime disaster waiting for weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Catch the papers! Stamp the truth before it migrates south!
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: You recovered the pages. The landlord's chronology has capsized beautifully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The page is damaged, but so is our dignity, and both remain usable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The complaint record has become weather. Fortunately, we still have witnesses and spite.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Back to the case board. Tie the facts down before they roll off the deck.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A board. Good. Ships need maps. Cases need boards. Panic needs walls to bounce off.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That board is less a chart and more a decorative fog bank.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The case has shape. Not a beautiful shape, but one that floats.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Look at that. Notice, rule, facts, proportionality. A fleet in formation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: To Housing Court. Remember: the home is not cargo. It is the reason the voyage matters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Beige should not be allowed to sound that confident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The rule is wobbling. Excellent. A bad rule makes poor ballast.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The complaints are sinking under their own dates. Beautiful. Procedural shipwreck, but theirs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Proportionality. That is the big wave. Stand straight and do not look at me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: He keeps his home. The ship is still ridiculous, but today it carried someone safely.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A housing victory! Excellent! Do housing victories pay? Asking as treasurer of panic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The poster has multiplied. Rights are contagious. Someone budget for tape.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That filing was not due yet. Why is the printer announcing storms before the clouds arrive?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The printer printed a blank page, then a page saying no. That is either malfunction or mutiny below deck.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: I am not saying it is sentient. I am saying it has chosen a dramatic moment to resemble a hostile crewman.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Find out why our filings are vanishing before the court treats us like a ship that forgot its own anchor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A deadline notice. Splendid. The iceberg has sent a calendar invitation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: This pleading looks like it survived a storm, a gull, and an argument with a stapler.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Call Bajtek. If the ship is leaking through a server rack, I want a man who owns cable ties.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: when Bajtek says printers are not sentient
text: That is exactly what a printer would want its technical counsel to say.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The Server Room is behind the archive corridor. Enter carefully. Machinery smells fear and toner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Find the logs. Logs are ship diaries, except they lie less and smell worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Repeated redirection. So the printer is not mad; someone moved the current.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Contradiction spotting. Excellent. Like checking whether the lighthouse is blinking or lying.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The Print Shop is in the Business District. Follow the smell of expensive toner and moral compromise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: These machines could print a fleet, sink it, and invoice the survivors.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: An invoice! The paper trail has floated to the surface wearing Szpon's perfume.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: He says no connection with the calm of a man standing on the dock holding our anchor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The denial has taken on water. Excellent. Continue poking the hull.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The logs point back inside the office. I hate internal leaks. They make the captain look architectural.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Why does our basement look like the bilge of a ship that lost a custody battle with furniture?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A hidden device. So it was sabotage. The printer may require an apology and possibly counselling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Electronic filing is compromised. Therefore the emergency scooter is now a legal vessel. I hate modernity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Clean delivery! The filing reached harbour with its dignity mostly dry.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The filing has tyre marks, but so do many great legal traditions, probably.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: It arrived late, bent, and spiritually damp. Argue sabotage before anyone asks about pigeons.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: To court. We have logs, invoices, wires, and the haunted honour of a wrongfully accused printer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The judge accepts the pattern. The current has been charted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Szpon's ship has appeared on our map carrying our printer maintenance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The filing lives. The deadline did not become a trapdoor. Bless every plank.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We doubted you. We feared you. We considered replacing you with a fax machine, which was grief speaking.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: It forgives us. The vessel has a soul, or at least excellent timing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Out of cyan. Of course. The sea always asks for more blue.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Something from Swine. With a bill attached. That is not correspondence; that is a cannonball in an envelope.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The printer is calm, the office is alive, and for once the ship is not screaming through the floorboards.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: International arbitration papers. That is a storm with a passport.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: He returns with goggles. Goggles! The co-captain abandoned ship and came back dressed as a recreational avalanche.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Mostly cultural? That is what people say before the invoice learns three languages.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Defend Mr. Swine, save the firm, and determine whether hospitality has become a torpedo.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That number has more decks than our entire ship. I can hear the hull reconsidering employment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Contract, authority, charges, translation. Four storms circling one man in ski goggles.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Question Swine slowly. If cornered too efficiently, he becomes decorative fog.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: He is not answering. He is sailing around the question in a small luxurious boat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Airport Road is open. Recover luggage, customs forms, and whatever dignity Swine checked in separately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: No commercial intent. Finally, a sentence from Swine's trip that lowers the waterline.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Go to the Japanese Embassy. Politely. The comedy target is Swine, not the harbour authorities of language.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: So politeness may not be a contract. I have never loved grammar more.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Swine's apartment is on the map. I am not emotionally prepared for its furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: This is not an apartment. This is where invoices go to become confident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: These expense reports were assembled during turbulence by a man losing a duel with columns.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The draft has no scary payment clause. A plank! A plank appears above the waves!
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Why is there a rights-case transfer hidden under ski expenses? Swine, what ocean have you been sailing?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We must reconstruct his memory by skiing. Law has finally left the harbour.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: He remembers the memorandum. Excellent. The fog lifts, and regrettably Swine remains visible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The memory is mostly snow, panic, and one important clipboard. We have won worse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The memory crashed into a snowman but returned carrying fragments. Very on brand.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Dr. A. Cula argues law. Swine explains facts. I sit behind them and age in nautical increments.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Do not let Swine interpret the contract. He once described a receipt as aspirational.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Politeness is not a contract. The ship turns. Slightly. Gracefully? No. But turns.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Travel, hospitality, confusion. Not authority. The foghorn of law is sounding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Seventeen puddings is not hospitality. It is dessert artillery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Ask them to dismiss the claim against the firm. Leave wisdom for another tribunal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Claim dismissed. The ship lives, and Swine may keep only a small personal pudding scandal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We are not liable. Swine is partially liable to common sense, but that court is always sitting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Alive but invoiced. I know this condition intimately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: You secretly funded pro-bono work from abroad by behaving exactly like a man destroying us?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That is not a confession. That is a fog bank wearing a halo.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A citywide notice. Citywide! The whole harbour is taking on water.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Listen. Quiet. The ship is not sinking. I distrust this silence deeply.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Hundreds of notices. The harbour itself has been served.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: They cannot close us. We have clients, debts, and at least one printer with emotional continuity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Continuing legal-social utility. That sounds complimentary if one ignores the cannon pointed at our office.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Prepare the final brief. Save the district. Keep this absurd vessel from being scrapped by committee.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Automated notices, filtered objections, public interest fog. The entire sea has become paperwork.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Go to Protest Square. If the notices hurt actual people, the Court must hear more than the city's brochure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: So many notices. So many people. This is not administration; this is a paper storm with shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Impact statements. Heavy file. Good. Consequences should have weight.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Waldek again. When one saved home appears at a protest, listen to the creak of the whole district.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: If Kowalski read the notice six times and became less certain, the notice has failed civilisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: City Hall has the plan. Put on your best shoes; we are sailing into polished fog.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: This building smells like fresh paint over old consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Access to justice hub, while removing everyone providing access to justice. The plan is a lifeboat that eats passengers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Identical notices for different lives. Individual assessment has been thrown overboard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The same people who print the storm sell the umbrellas. I dislike this business model intensely.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: after city official says individually assessed
text: Individually assessed? Then every individual was apparently born with the same printer settings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Find the objection system. Bring Bajtek and emotional distance. This is not a metaphor anymore.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That is not a printer. That is a battleship with trays.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Objections marked duplicate before review. The ship did not miss the harbour; someone moved the harbour.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: One button, hundreds of consequences. Efficiency has become a cannon.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: All hands on deck. Dodge the paper, stamp the truth, and do not let bureaucracy reload.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Clean shutdown. Objections recovered. The battleship is drifting and I am prepared to call that justice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The printer is stopped. We are bruised, toner-marked, and technically alive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Bajtek shut it down. Fewer objections survived, but enough floated up to testify.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: office printer prints I WARNED YOU
text: It warned us. We ignored the small printer and met the dreadnought.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A pro-bono ledger hidden under ski expenses. Swine has weaponised irresponsibility into charity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: You secretly saved the ship by behaving like a man drilling holes in it?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Ridiculous. Reckless. Possibly noble. I hate that all three are true.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Assemble the crew. This is no longer one lawyer at the rail; this is the whole vessel turning into the storm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Community morale? Yes. I can do morale. I have been panicking publicly for years.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That team arrangement is less a crew and more a lifeboat argument.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The brief has a crew, a map, and only moderate screaming. Excellent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Look at us. Witnesses, evidence, law, printer trauma. A fleet under one flag.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: To the Supreme Court. Bring the brief, the ledger, the logs, and everyone who still believes this ship deserves harbour.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: This room is too grand for panic. I shall panic internally, which is healthier for decorum.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The Court sees the notice problem. The first leak is on the record.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Public interest is not a spell. Good. Someone write that on the mast.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Automation did not remove discretion. It hid it in the cargo hold.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The contractor profits when the noticed people disappear. Even I can hear the alarm bell over the waves.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Say what we are, Dr. A. Cula. Not efficient. Not polished. Open.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We are not efficient. But people come here when efficient systems have already failed them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We are not the best firm in Warsaw. But we are, regrettably, someone's last firm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: A polished ship may refuse desperate passengers to protect the carpets. Ours lets them through the door and apologises for the leak.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The scheme is suspended. Objections restored. The harbour breathes. I may never recover financially from this joy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: We live. The district lives. Some storms remain, but the ship is not being dismantled today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Temporary injunction. Thin, breathing, and absolutely welcome aboard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Friends, colleagues, printer, we gather upon the deck of survival—
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: You prepared a postcard while physically present? That is not correspondence. That is evasive theatre.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Welcome back, counsel. The printer recognises us. I am choosing not to question the theology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Low paper. Of course. Even victory has office supplies.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Mr. Pig: still panicking, now with a budget. This is civilisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: route blocker commented by Pig after court route unlock
text: North to court, south to financial ruin. Naturally we go north.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Not that binder. That one contains historical invoices. Open it and we may discover new species of debt.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: if Pig comments on café from office phone
text: Café Paragraf charges extra for oat milk and atmosphere. We can afford neither, but morale demands sacrifice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Financial Crisis v. Basic Procedural Competence. I object to being named so accurately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: if Pig accompanies to Tenants Row
text: Every balcony is a tiny legal deck. Imagine the notices falling like rain. Actually, do not imagine that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That plant is alive. Good. The apartment has precedent for survival.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: He speaks like a man who thinks compassion violates the building code.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: after Grzyb says rights must be beige
text: Rights exercised in beige are still rights, but I resent the upholstery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: That cable is blinking with confidence. No object in this firm has earned confidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The toner budget in this room could refloat three medium firms and one small harbour.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Why is there a box labelled URGENT 2011? Did we ignore a storm until it fossilised?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Ordinary incompetence is our jurisdiction. This was foreign interference.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Every arrival board is a list of people with better explanations than Swine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Stand up straight. We require clarity, not international embarrassment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Those goggles have seen more funding than our copier.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: when arbitrator warns against ski metaphors
text: No ski metaphors. Good. I have several boat metaphors trembling in reserve.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: If even the café is threatened, where will lawyers panic at retail prices?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The minutes say consultation occurred. That is generous for a meeting with no ears.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: It fires red tape. The metaphor has developed artillery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The Final Brief is filed. If paper could stand at attention, this stack would salute.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 1 idle line as Cula enters; thrown into the air, then snagged by Cula's arrival
text: It would appear that we are taking on... ah. Dr. A. Cula.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; opening office crisis; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 crisis reveal; formal panic opener before rent claim
text: The firm is not bankrupt. Bankruptcy has paperwork. We are currently in the pre-paperwork screaming phase.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; rent claim only in this beat; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 crisis reveal; rent claim focal line
text: The rent is taking on water.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; rent claim only in this beat; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 crisis reveal; six-week overdue rent specificity
text: Six weeks overdue. Six weeks below the surface, and the landlord is knocking on the hull with a very calm fist.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; rent claim only in this beat; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 crisis reveal; rhetorical question answered by Pig
text: Can a firm sail while six weeks underwater? Apparently yes, because here we are, damp and accepting your help.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; rent claim only in this beat; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 crisis reveal; office object framed through rent crisis
text: The door is still on its hinges. For a rented room this far underwater, I consider the hinges hostile witnesses.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; rent claim only in this beat; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 crisis reveal; panic precludes case explanation
text: I would brief you properly, but the deck is slanting and I have misplaced the part of myself that knows facts.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; Pig does not explain the case; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 deflection toward Murrow and quest pointer
text: Mr. Murrow has... Mr. Murrow knows... please ask Mr. Murrow before I brief you using buckets.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 draft; defers case explanation to Mr. Murrow; not final committed game dialogue.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 1 V1.1 pass 2 — Pig idle muttering as Cula approaches case board
text: It would appear that we are...
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; sentence collapses on seeing Cula; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 V1.1 pass 2 — opening line of Pig's rent monologue
text: Dr. A. Cula. Welcome. You arrive during a moment.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; composure recovers for one sentence; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: The firm is not bankrupt. Bankruptcy requires paperwork, and our paperwork is currently underwater along with the rest of us.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; canonical Pig negation+justification+comic reframing; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 V1.1 pass 2 — direct rent statement
text: The rent is six weeks overdue.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; one item only; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 V1.1 pass 2 — maritime extension; landlord knock
text: Six weeks under the surface. The landlord knocks on the hull. Politely. The polite knock is somehow worse than a loud one.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; maritime metaphor snags on rent; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
text: Can a firm sail while six weeks underwater? I have asked myself this. The honest answer appears to be yes, on momentum.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; essayistic tail clause cut per pack §I; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 V1.1 pass 2 — closing maritime image
text: So. We float. In the way that boats with leaks float, which is to say, less every morning.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; maritime closing; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 2 V1.1 pass 2 — fragmentary deflection to Murrow
text: Mr. Murrow has the folder. Mr. Murrow knows the— ask Mr. Murrow.
notes: Phase 7 V1.1 pass 2 draft; interruption is the deflection; one em-dash budgeted; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 8 V1.3 pass 3 — drift-by aside about printer lease
text: The printer lease has come back as a kraken. We renew next month, presumably with bait.
notes: Phase 7 V1.3 pass 3 draft; printer-lease only (no rent / no retainer); not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 8 V1.3 pass 3 — apprentice/panic lecture during readiness check
text: When I was an apprentice, we had no readiness check. Only fear. We navigated by panic alone.
notes: Phase 7 V1.3 pass 3 draft; canonical 'navigate' usage per pack §A.5; three sentences; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 8 brief intrusion; turn 1 — panic register; maritime imagery (overboard); addresses Murrow, not Halina
text: Mr. Murrow. The Hennessy retainer phone has gone overboard. Third time this morning. Where is Asia.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 8 brief intrusion; turn 2 — panic register; maritime imagery (small craft warning); cut off by Murrow's shepherding
text: And the printer is throwing red lights. Small craft warning, Mr. Murrow —
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 13 V1.7 pass 5 — threshold; briefly-paternal one-beat warmth as Pig registers the team's return
text: You are back. Good. Good.
notes: Phase 7 V1.7 pass 5 commit shape; briefly-paternal threshold beat; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 13 V1.7 pass 5 — H4 firm-first / client-second / return-to-anxiety celebration paragraph; named-items list (rent / printer / Sikorska fee)
text: We have come through another impossible thing. The client — yes, the client — also survived. The rent will breathe; the printer can wait; the 5,000 PLN Sikorska fee came through.
notes: Phase 7 V1.7 pass 5 commit shape; H4 celebration paragraph; two canonical em-dashes flanking 'yes, the client'; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 13 V1.7 pass 5 — missing-Swine-retainer aside; third financial claim; maritime / sincere-panic register; return-to-anxiety beat
text: Mr. Swine's retainer is still — somewhere. Sea of Japan. Presumably alive.
notes: Phase 7 V1.7 pass 5 commit shape; canonical em-dash in 'is still — somewhere'; third financial claim; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 13 V1.7 pass 5 — distractable-anxious exit beat; Pig drifts back toward his office
text: Yes. Yes, the next thing.
notes: Phase 7 V1.7 pass 5 commit shape; distractable-anxious exit; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 14 V1.7 pass 5 — Pig reads the Swine postcard body aloud; cheerful-evasive Swine text in Pig's voice
text: Greetings from Sapporo. A very serious gentleman in the lobby has proposed a venture involving conference centers and a fisheries contact, and I have given him my card on principle. Keep Pig & Swine afloat in my absence.
notes: Phase 7 V1.7 pass 5 commit shape; Beat 14 read-aloud; text is Swine's postcard body; not final.

speaker: Mr. Pig
context: Beat 14 V1.7 pass 5 — postcard reaction line; maritime continuity from retainer aside; distractable-anxious register
text: The Sea of Japan, then.
notes: Phase 7 V1.7 pass 5 commit shape; Beat 14 reaction; Sea of Japan continuity from retainer aside; not final.
